title: us-west-2 cooling degradation (22 minutes)
summary: A cooling loop controller in LUM-HIL-1 rebooted into a diagnostic mode, throttling compute in us-west-2 for 22 minutes. No data loss; roughly 9% of customer workloads saw elevated latency.
what_we_said: We published exact minute counts and the controller firmware version, and committed to N+1 cooling controllers by year-end.
resolution: N+1 controllers installed August 2024. Automatic 10% credits applied to affected accounts. Postmortem published unedited.

timeline.event: Cooling controller reboots into diagnostic mode.
Beacon thermal alerts fire; compute throttling begins.
Status page notice posted (within COM-9 window).
Controller manually restarted; temperatures normalize.
Throttling lifted; incident closed at 22 minutes of degradation.

title: Personal tweet posted from corporate account ('my landlord is a demon')
summary: A marketing intern, logged into the corporate account on a personal phone, posted 'my landlord is a demon' to 210k followers. It reached 340k likes before deletion and remains our most-liked post ever.
what_we_said: 'That one was not scheduled. The intern is fine, the landlord dispute is real, and we're reviewing our posting controls.' The internet respected the honesty.
resolution: Two-approver rule adopted for all corporate posts. The intern was not disciplined and later hired full-time. The landlord reportedly fixed the boiler.

timeline.event: Tweet posted from corporate account.
Comms on-call notices unusual engagement velocity.
Tweet deleted; screenshot already viral.
Follow-up statement posted; two-approver rule drafted the next morning.

title: Glowworm staging logs data-handling scare
summary: A Glowworm staging environment was found writing customer account display names into debug logs. Initial fear of a PII exposure; forensic review confirmed logs never left the private network and contained no credentials or payment data.
what_we_said: We notified potentially affected enterprise customers within 72 hours, before the forensic review completed, and committed to quarterly data-handling audits.
resolution: No exposure confirmed. Debug logging scrubbed via redaction library. Quarterly data-handling audits running since Q2 2025. EU regulator correspondence closed without action (see LP-006).

timeline.event: Engineer flags display names in staging debug logs.
Incident declared; forensic review begins.
Enterprise customers notified within the 72-hour window.
Forensic review confirms no external exposure.
Redaction library deployed across all Glowworm environments.

title: us-east-1 partial outage (12 minutes)
summary: A cable tray power path in LUM-ASH-1 (Ashburn) briefly failed, taking part of us-east-1 offline for 12 minutes. The postmortem identified that power path as a single point of failure and opened remediation ticket FAC-2291, scheduled for Q3 2026.
what_we_said: CTO Theo Lindgren published the raw postmortem, typos included, and promised 'multiple redundant safeguards' on the affected power path.
resolution: Postmortem earned public goodwill. Automatic credits applied. FAC-2291 (the actual redundancy work) was scheduled for Q3 2026 and had not been executed as of July 2026 — the phrase 'multiple redundant safeguards' described the plan, not the installed state.

timeline.event: Power path on Ashburn cable tray row C fails; partial us-east-1 outage begins.
Beacon pages on-call; failover absorbs most traffic.
Status page notice posted.
Power path restored via secondary feed; service normal at 12 minutes.
Postmortem published promising 'multiple redundant safeguards'; FAC-2291 opened and scheduled for Q3 2026.

title: Invoice decimal error (customers charged 100x)
summary: A billing pipeline change shifted a decimal, and 1,912 customers received invoices for 100x their actual usage. One startup was invoiced $2.4M for a month of $24k usage; their CFO's screenshot went viral.
what_we_said: 'The decimal moved. Your money did not — no cards were charged. Corrected invoices within 24 hours.' We avoided promising 'full refunds' since nothing had been collected.
resolution: Corrected invoices issued in 19 hours. Invoice review gate added: any run where median invoice value shifts >10% halts for human review. Class action settled in 2026 with no admission of wrongdoing (see LP-002).

timeline.event: Billing run dispatches 1,912 inflated invoices.
Support tickets spike; viral CFO screenshot posted.
Billing run halted; no payment collection had occurred.
All corrected invoices re-issued (19 hours).
Invoice review gate deployed to production billing.

title: Lumi replies to customers exclusively in haiku
summary: A Lumi prompt experiment escaped staging and for six hours the AI support agent answered every ticket in strict 5-7-5 haiku, including a P1 database escalation ('Your cluster is down / We see it, we are so sorry / Spring will come again').
what_we_said: 'Lumi got poetic. The tickets were real, the haiku were unauthorized, and every affected customer has been re-answered in prose.'
resolution: Canary policy adopted: every Lumi prompt deploy must first run against 50 sandbox tickets. Several customers requested haiku mode as a feature; request declined by legal.

timeline.event: Experimental prompt promoted to production by mistagged config.
First customer compliments 'the new support poetry.'
Support lead flags P1 haiku; rollback executed in one click.
All haiku-answered tickets re-queued to human agents.
50-ticket canary requirement added to Lumi deploy policy.

title: Kombucha keg over-pressurization near-miss
summary: Keg #3 of the Fermentation Friday program began 'hissing ominously' after a double-fermented ginger batch. Facilities evacuated the kitchenette as a precaution; the keg was walked to the loading dock and vented safely.
what_we_said: Internal-only comms: 'The keg has been talked down. Ginger batch retired.' No public statement was needed; one employee tweeted about it anyway (4k likes).
resolution: Pressure-relief valves rated to vent at 42 PSI installed on all kegs. A 'kombucha steward' rotation was added to Facilities duty. CFO Hank Bream continued taste-testing.

timeline.event: Keg #3 begins audible hissing; kitchenette cleared.
Facilities escorts keg to loading dock on a hand truck.
Keg vented safely; ginger batch retired.
42 PSI pressure-relief valves installed fleet-wide (all six kegs).

title: AirDrop drone lands in HQ fountain during press demo
summary: During a press demo of the Lumina AirDrop swag-delivery pilot, unit 1 misread a reflective surface, identified the HQ fountain as its landing pad, and delivered itself plus 40 stress balls into two feet of water in front of three journalists.
what_we_said: 'The drone found water. The stress balls float. We are recalibrating.' Coverage was mocking but affectionate.
resolution: FAA warning letter received and answered with an accepted corrective plan (see LP-007): geofencing update plus a two-person waypoint review requirement. Unit 1 repaired; stress balls recovered.

timeline.event: Press demo begins on HQ lawn.
Unit 1 deviates to fountain; delivers payload into water.
Journalist applause; footage posted within minutes.
FAA warning letter received.
Corrective plan accepted: geofence update + two-person waypoint review.
